req.title: Fraud alert response (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-063 defines a fraud-alert confirmation flow with a 24-hour window. Key partitions are: (1) valid acknowledgement within window -> alert closed, card reissued if fraud confirmed; (2) denial within window -> alert closed, no reissue; (3) response after window expires -> 410 Gone; (4) account auto-freeze when unacknowledged. Negative paths required by the requirement include unauthorised access, exceeded limits, and insufficient funds. BVA targets the 24-hour boundary (just-inside vs just-outside). STC covers the state machine: ACTIVE -> CLOSED or ACTIVE -> FROZEN. DT covers the confirm/deny decision matrix. NEG covers expired alert (410), unauthorized caller, and invalid payload. Violations fixed: test[6] was negative+STC -> changed to negative+NEG; test[7] was negative+EP -> changed to negative+NEG.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status ACTIVE and id stored in <alert_id_fixture>
Alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "confirm"} using valid customer auth token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-063: customer confirms suspected-fraud alert
R-FIN-063: alert closed on confirmation
R-FIN-063: card reissued when fraud confirmed
STC: state transition ACTIVE -> CLOSED (fraud confirmed path)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(
    active_alert_id, auth_token, base_url
):
    url = f"{base_url}/alerts/{active_alert_id}"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {"action": "confirm"}

    resp = requests.patch(url, json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED"
    assert body["card_reissued"] is True

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED"

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status ACTIVE and id stored in <alert_id_fixture>
Alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "deny"} using valid customer auth token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-063: customer denies suspected-fraud alert
R-FIN-063: alert closed on denial
R-FIN-063: card NOT reissued when fraud denied
DT: decision table row - action=deny, within window -> CLOSED, no reissue

tests.name: test_response_to_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists whose 24-hour window has elapsed (status FROZEN or EXPIRED)
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "confirm"} using valid customer auth token against expired alert
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-063: unacknowledged alerts auto-freeze account
R-FIN-063: error pathway - 410 when alert expired
NEG: negative path - response submitted after 24-hour deadline

tests.name: test_unauthorised_caller_cannot_acknowledge_alert
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status ACTIVE
No valid auth token is provided (missing or invalid Bearer token)
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "confirm"} with no Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error indicating missing or invalid credentials
trace: R-FIN-063: negative path - unauthorised actor attempts acknowledgement
R-FIN-063 category: auth
NEG: negative path - missing authentication credentials

tests.name: test_alert_response_at_exactly_24_hour_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: A fraud alert exists whose creation timestamp is exactly 24 hours ago minus 1 second (just inside the valid window)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 OK (request just inside 24-hour window accepted)
trace: R-FIN-063: customer must confirm within 24 hours
BVA: boundary at T=24h; last valid moment is T=24h - epsilon
BVA: on-point (just inside boundary) must succeed

tests.name: test_alert_response_one_second_past_24_hour_boundary_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists whose creation timestamp is exactly 24 hours ago plus 1 second (just outside the valid window)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 410 Gone (request just outside 24-hour window rejected)
trace: R-FIN-063: unacknowledged alerts auto-freeze after 24 hours
BVA: boundary at T=24h; first invalid moment is T=24h + epsilon
BVA: off-point (just outside boundary) must fail with 410

tests.name: test_exceeded_rate_limit_on_alert_acknowledgement_returns_429
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status ACTIVE
Rate limit threshold for PATCH /alerts/{id} is known (e.g. 5 requests per minute)
actions: Send PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "confirm"} repeatedly until rate limit is exceeded
Capture HTTP response status and body on the request that exceeds the limit
expected: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests on the exceeding request
Response body contains error indicating rate limit exceeded
Alert status remains ACTIVE until a successful acknowledgement
trace: R-FIN-063: negative path - exceeded limits error pathway
NEG: negative path - rate limit exceeded on alert acknowledgement endpoint

tests.name: test_insufficient_funds_does_not_block_alert_acknowledgement
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status ACTIVE for an account with zero or insufficient balance
Account balance is zero or below minimum threshold
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"action": "confirm"} using valid customer auth token for an account with insufficient funds
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 OK (alert acknowledgement is independent of account balance)
No insufficient-funds error blocks the fraud alert acknowledgement flow
trace: R-FIN-063: negative path - insufficient funds account must still be able to acknowledge fraud alert
NEG: negative path - insufficient funds condition does not impede alert confirmation
